text.title: Pulse Survey — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for the 4-question morale pulse, anonymity, alert thresholds, and PM SLA.

rule.statement: The pulse asks EXACTLY 4 questions, no exceptions: (Q1) eNPS "How likely are you to recommend this team as a place to work? 0-10", (Q2) workload 1-10, (Q3) autonomy 1-10, (Q4) clarity 1-10. Adding a 5th question drops response rate ~12pp per added question.

rationale.source: Officevibe Pulse research, Tinypulse cohort data

rule.rationale: Response rate is the dominant variable. 4 questions hit a 75-85% completion rate; 7+ questions drop to 30-50%. Trend signal requires consistent response rate.

rule.statement: Aggregate results MUST be hidden until ≥ 3 respondents AND ≥ 60% of team has responded. Team size < 5 → suppress per-axis median, report only "responded / not responded" counts.

rationale.source: Differential privacy practice / Gallup small-group recommendation

rule.rationale: N-of-1 or N-of-2 reports are de-anonymising; team members can identify whose answer drove the alert. Suppression rule is the social contract that keeps response rate sustainable.

rule.statement: An alert fires when ANY of: (a) eNPS aggregate dropped > 20 points in 1 sprint, (b) median on any axis < 6, (c) response rate dropped below 50% (silent disengagement signal). Alert triggers a manager 1:1 with each team member within 48 business hours.

rationale.source: Gallup Q12 alert-threshold studies + Lencioni distressed-team patterns

rule.rationale: eNPS drop > 20 is the leading indicator of attrition by 6-12 weeks. Median < 6 means > 30% of team is unhappy. Response-rate collapse precedes both.

rule.statement: If alert fired, PM MUST schedule (and complete) 1:1s with all team members within 48 BUSINESS HOURS of alert. If 1:1s cannot happen, the SURVEY MUST PAUSE until the PM is ready to act. Running pulse without acting destroys trust faster than not running pulse.
rule.rationale: Teams that experience "feedback theatre" (asked, no action) churn 2.5× faster than teams not surveyed at all. The pause rule prevents anti-survey damage.

rule.statement: Survey aggregate must NEVER tie scores back to individuals, even when team size + technical channel make it possible. PM must publicly state the no-retaliation policy at every pulse launch and treat any 1:1 information as confidential.

rationale.source: OSHA whistleblower protections / EEOC retaliation guidance

rule.rationale: Retaliation kills the system AND has legal exposure. The verbal commitment is part of the methodology, not an HR add-on.

example.title: Pulse text (verbatim)
example.code:
1) On a scale of 0-10, how likely are you to recommend this team as a place to work? (eNPS)
2) On a scale of 1-10, how is your workload this week? (1 = drowning, 10 = ideal)
3) On a scale of 1-10, how much autonomy do you have over what you work on?
4) On a scale of 1-10, how clear are the goals for this sprint?
Optional comment box: "anything you want me to know?"
example.note: ~60 seconds, single question per axis. Comment box is OPTIONAL — required comment box would drop response rate.

example.title: Survey scope creep (r1 violation)
example.code:
1) eNPS 2) workload 3) autonomy 4) clarity 5) "Do you feel valued by your manager?" 6) "How is your relationship with peers?" 7) "How do you rate the company benefits?"
7 questions. Response rate drops to ~40%. Trend data is noise. PM cannot tell whether morale changed or sample changed.
example.note: r1 cap is hard. New axes go in a quarterly engagement survey, not the weekly pulse.
